hey p, i know i've been a bad friend, but can you really blame me? maybe you can. i've had my own errors and faults din naman throughout the entire course of whatever we had. i'm sorry because things had to end between us 'that' way. college was fast approaching and i knew to myself that it would put an inevitable distance between us. truthfully, [REDACTED_NAME] ibbring up [REDACTED_NAME] tayo in the future. if there was even a picture of us in your own little version of the 'future'. if [REDACTED_NAME] future mo, not just as your friend. or if the vision you had for your future still involved being married to a man who can give you your own children because coming out to your family was never really an option. i mean who am i even kidding? you would always talk about getting married someday and having kids of your own even though we had an ongoing thing. parehas [REDACTED_NAME] that can't be achieved if we truly stayed together. [REDACTED_NAME], p. i can't be the man [REDACTED_NAME] sa parents mo. i can't be the man who will hold your hand and smile at you once you reach the altar. when you questioned me about us being 'exclusive' i had to force down the bitter thoughts to myself. [REDACTED_NAME] tayo permanent. eventually, you'd need to dispose of me and settle down with a man. i was already at the acceptance stage even before everything came crashing down. fast forward, our silly little thing ended and soon after you got yourself a boyfriend. in the rare times we talk, you always mention that he treats you well. i'm happy for you, p. ever since you guys got together, the time we spent talking to each other lessened. we both agreed to stay friends despite our past. but i'm sorry p because i couldn’t honor that promise. the distance put between us was intentional. somehow it felt wrong to engage even though platonic nalang tayo. i guess it's the guilt and the regret because that could've been me by your side if only i were a man. i don't even know what pushed me in the first place to suddenly say all of this. maybe it's because of the fact na our thing ended right before college and now, [REDACTED_NAME]graduate. yet throughout all these years, i carried these thoughts close to myself. unpsoken and buried far behind at the back of my mind. you'll always have a special place within me, p. pero i'm afraid we can't be the same anymore. we've both outgrown each other, p. as friends and as individuals. i'm sorry p for being a bad friend. for not talking to you anymore. for getting upset when you forgot about my birthday. you know, we should really stop greeting each other on our birthdays, p. maybe it's for the better.

Hello everyone, please hear me out on my problem. I've been talking to a fellow Isko from [REDACTED_NAME]. He's very nice to talk with, and we shared a lot of sexts and flirty messages. We planned to meet up but he said he had to go to his province na for Christmas break so it was cancelled. Anyway, we chatted some more and I propised the idea of FUBU/FWB kasi honestly the idea of meeting up with randoms every time scares me a bit + it's safer that way. I was shy pa nga kasi he asked me if we will be exclusive kung ganun, and sabi [REDACTED_NAME] he wants to explore pa. We eventually agreed to be exclusive FWB. We chatted for a few days, even exchanged more flirty texts, and I can say that we vibed with our horniness and also our personalities (kahit there's little material to work with hehe). As far as I knew, we were FWB and we plan to meet in January (and have some fun :)). Then, when I woke up one morning, I found out that he blocked me on Telegram suddenly, and I did not know why or what I did wrong or what happened between us. I was sure that we ended our conversation the previous night on good terms, but the next day I was already blocked. I tried to approach him and talk to him on his other socials but he also blocked me there na. To this day, I'm still blocked on his accounts. What went wrong? I'm not trying to force anyone to do anything, but I just want to know what happened between us and [REDACTED_NAME]block. Our conversations went well and we even had a plan sa January when we finally meet up. Is this normal [REDACTED_NAME]/FWB scene? Am I too soft for this? [REDACTED_NAME] confirmation or story on why he doesn't want to talk to me anymore. I can accept if he does not like me anymore but I want to hear it directly from him :(( I'm sorry if I sound too obsessed but it's very rare for someone to talk to me and show interest so that's probably why I get attached and hurt too easily. I should probably uninstall [REDACTED_NAME]. To R (i'll use Blackbox as your nickname since I saw on [REDACTED_NAME] that you wanted to be invited there), if you're seeing this, you can chat me, I'm still waiting for you. You might never see this but on the chance that you do, please send me a chat and we can say our goodbyes formally. Or if something happened and you cannot talk to me for the time being, I'm willing to wait until January so that I will not pressure you to talk when you don't want to. If you don't want to talk to me anymore, please tell me so I can leave you alone and I can finally have peace of mind. Thank you for chatting with me even if it was short-lived. - Reiner

i don't know when this will get posted. maybe it will, maybe it won't. i don't even know if i hope you'll see this: i honestly just feel like screaming into the void. so, as of writing this now, i still love you. i don't know if it'll fade anytime soon. i don't know if i ever will. you're my first love, and nothing changes that. i would've married you, you know? if you had made it so i could stay, if you had changed your mind that day and apologized and given it another shot, if you'd given yourself the chance to change, then...maybe someday, we would've. i'm sitting here writing this and thinking of everything we were. remember how brightly you used to look at me? you never saw it yourself, maybe because you never believed you could hold such beauty, but i swear i saw the stars in your eyes. i'd've loved you until every hair on our heads was grey, until our voices wore out and died forever from years of banter, and until our hands grew numb and tired from holding each other. i'd've loved you until our bones were picked clean somewhere deep in the earth, when i had no more heart to hold my love for you. do you know that? i know you do. i don't know why i'm bothering to ask. you loved me too. we always said so. you told me you'd be glad to call me your wife. oh, my love, i would've killed to be. i wanted you to be my husband. i wanted a life together. but that's not how things turned out. and you know damn well whose fault it was. and now look at both of us, picking up the pieces of our hearts because you decided to break them again and again and again and again. the funny thing is, and you *still* know this, i would've stayed. i would've worked with you on it. fucking hell, you stupid idiot, i would've stayed. and i know you meant for letting me go to be an act of love. you thought, that maybe by releasing me from your arms, one of the only places in the whole world where i felt safe, i'd be free. except now i just feel lost. you, who found me beautiful even when i cried, who held me at my worst moments, who saw me in ways that i never let anyone else see, you are gone. how dare you leave me? how dare you? how dare you leave me when you promised we would weather all of this together? what am i supposed to do without my best friend, my partner, the only person i trusted with my secrets and my hurts? where is the love in all of this? and still i love you. i love you so much. and i don't know where to put that love without you here. it's not even my fault you're gone. i could take it if it was, because that means it's something for me to repair. but i can't do anything. there's nothing i can do except cry and mourn and beg that i wake up two-and-a-half years ago when it all began so i can relive it all with what i know now and maybe have it all go right. i know i'll live on. you used to love me for my strength and that has never once faded. it doesn't change that i wish you'd risen to meet it all with me. it doesn't change that i miss my person. it doesn't change that i keep looking over my shoulder, breathless, like i might see you there waiting for me with your arms open. i want to hear you tell me you're sorry, that you want to try again, and you'll change. you'll do it right this time. ...maybe someday we'll get to try again. someday when we're older and whatever's broken inside of you is fixed, we'll run into each other somewhere and we'll stop for a moment, just a little surprised, and we'll stumble over our words trying to fill the time and space in between until it flows as naturally as it used to. and we'll find that even if we're different now, we love each other still. you always said that exes getting back together was one of your favorite tropes, right? we always felt like soulmates. maybe it'll come true. i'm sorry the chapter had to close. i love you still. maybe someday, my love, my knight, my heart.

During our lessons, I find myself falling for him a little bit more, napopogian ako sa accent niya and how he is patient and soft-spoken as a person, especially since beginner [REDACTED_NAME]. He said he never got the chance to go into a real university early on so sophomore pa lang siya online, so he spent his time teaching people his native language. Of course, as time passed by, I find myself thinking of him all the time, when I practice the language alone, I dedicate my sentences to him, [REDACTED_NAME] siya "Tell me something in my language" hindi [REDACTED_NAME] [REDACTED_NAME] because all I've been practicing is "I've been thinking of you" or "I love your precious smile" alangan [REDACTED_NAME] diba? Everything was okay, I was intact, until...he started to say affectionate sentences in his language. At first, I thought, maybe as a friend [REDACTED_NAME], maybe expressive [REDACTED_NAME]. I always shut it down though, or said "thank you" [REDACTED_NAME] awkwardly, I pretended not to know what it meant in English saying I "only picked up a few words", but it didn't mean that I did not feel the need to say it back, I was just pretty cautious as a person. I always kept it in, repressed the romantic feelings I had for him because there has not been sufficient time yet. One night though, and probably the last night we would talk properly, he mentioned his girlfriend ... I couldn't believe it "girlfriend?" I asked, and my stomach dropped, he has been describing me the same way he described her. I was genuinely scared, I backed off real quick, how he describes her so similarly to me, I knew somehow what he felt for me was not neutral and thats when it dawned on me, his compliments were NOT platonic at all. I didn't know what to do, I was petrified, sabi ko pa nga sa sarili ko "Jusko, ayaw [REDACTED_NAME] third party" . It doesn't remove the fact that I still like him, that for a moment, I contemplated what it was like being in his arms, for a moment, I imagined what it was like to be loved. But i know I could not make another woman cry, so I did not act on my impulses that night. I ghosted him for days muna, I needed time to think, about what I'm going to say. It hit hard for me because in the past, my ex got with another woman immediately, even if she knew of what happened, they still did it to me nevertheless, I knew what it felt like to be the "gf". Anyways I came back after days, of course, it did not sit well with him that I disappeared and he started being angry over my "attitude" and because of the things I asked afterward. He said that he doesn't know if he can trust me again and starting from now on, I'll only talk to him when I need a lesson. Of course that hurt me, I tried to de-escalate the situation, but he wouldn't listen, so of course, [REDACTED_NAME]. Lol now I'm grieving it because I genuinely liked him, but I knew better than to put my own desires just to get with a guy. I've honestly been just distracting myself lately, I don't know. Hi Adel! Happy New Year. I know I should not be composing a long damn letter for you since [REDACTED_NAME] you wouldn't read this anyways. but hey, I want to tell you that I am grateful that u came to save me from the depths of my despair this year! I did not regret loving you but I really despised the version of you AFTER our break up. We both know the version of u that truly loved me will, and would never do that awful shit to me. I guess I wasn't that ready to witness your change — maybe I did put up my hopes on you that u r not capable of hurting me which led us to blame and throw dirt to each other. We ignored the signs, covered up the stains, and pretended that everything was okay - when it is not. I loved you and I guess I will yearn for you way longer than I had you. Sorry for all the things that I had done that caused you your sanity. Thank you for the 7 fruitful months of temporary love, affection, & lessons that will surely be carried as I embark on a new journey without you. Surely I can't hate you because at some point, you showed me something that got me thinking you were the man I'd thought I marry and will had a future with. How could I hate the person I loved relentlessly? How could I unlove the person who made me feel that love still exists? How could those black eyes that once stared at me full of love can change in a flicker of light? These questions will remain unanswered and I am wise enough to not seek an answer - to quietly sit here, embracing the fragments of our love that u had left. I heard you're in a better place na and I won't be reaching out. It was great to know you and to be known by you. Good luck, babe— —or so I would like to say, but I know full well that we had (rather, what we COULD have had) had been nipped at the bud by none other than myself, because I was young (both of us were, really; and I know na dwelling on something that occurred back when we were in elementary is most definitely something, but I can't help it—I see her everywhere I go, and I see her in ME, in the way I belatedly realized that so much of me is a product of her friendship, her love), I was naive, I was a schoolgirl with misshapen braids and a Hello Kitty backpack, carrying with me nothing but the weight of several textbooks, confused feelings, and Catholic guilt. It goes without saying that an intense feeling of regret and melancholy invades my senses (and, perhaps also, the very core of my being) whenever my thoughts drift to my [REDACTED_NAME] (not her actual name, of course, but a reference to the beloved star in “The [REDACTED_NAME] and The Frog,” a film that both us highly favored). My first love was never T (a boy; one I had liked for a short time back in Grade 6, and one I often refer to as my first love)―it was her. And if that love was platonic, romantic, or something in between, I shall deign not to think about―because, to me, it was (and still is) love all the same. It’s strange, really―at that time, I had known nothing else but the expectations that had been set for me. You changed that; you changed everything; you made me realize that there was something to life beyond all those awards and titles and appearances that I had to maintain. Thinking about it now makes me feel sad, because we were literally just Grade 4 students back then―we were supposed to be children, not girls who clung too hard to fantasies (and to each other) to seek an escape from the life that was carved out for us. But let's go back to my Evangeline―like I said, she was my first love. Though I'm fond of declaring T as my muse (a running inside joke)―my ever-elusive muse, at that―I know, in the most locked up chambers of my heart, and in the deepest recesses of my brain, that the true muse, the true inspiration, is her. T was an ideal, someone whose actual traits I mixed in with what I perceived to be befitting a character bearing his likeness. Yes, I found thrill in the competition he (my academic rival at that time) provided, and a strange exhilaration in the way he carried himself, but the reasons why I "loved" him were mostly fabricated, products from my own imagination. I did not love him, I loved the thought of him. T was an ideal, my [REDACTED_NAME] was real. She was real, so beautifully real. And she kept me grounded, tethered to reality. Her voice, both sweet and honeyed, and warm and enthralling, seemed to be able to glide effortlessly towards notes that my own voice, untrained and used only for speaking, for declaring, could only hope to reach back then. But she held my hand all the same and told me that if there was someone that could join her in the choir, it was me. And so, I began to truly "sing" (cliché though it sounds), to write letters, to start drawing, to start finding myself. Her smile, as soft as the gentle touch of my dearest comfort blanket (hayst), made me smile. Her laugh made me laugh. Her sadness made me sad. Her happiness made me glad. Her love for everything, I loved in turn. She was, for lack of a better term, everything, so utterly perfect, even in the "imperfections" I claimed she had, back when we argued during that fatal moment when I ruined it all. Because who am I if not someone who destroys everything with her touch―with her thoughts? And, God, I know that we were only children back then―and children tend to do stupid (and I had been stupid, very, very stupid) things that they either forget about or remember for the rest of their life―and I know that we’re okay now (not as close as before, of course, since we went our separate ways after graduating elementary school, but close enough to talk from time to time and greet each other on our birthdays), but how I wish I could go back in time and try to redo our story. I loved you, my Evangeline―I loved you so much.

They’ll ask me why I love you, and they’ll expect an answer about the way you treat me or how you make me feel. But that’s not what I’ll tell them. I love you for who you are — for your hopes and dreams, your quiet ambitions, your hobbies and the things that light you up when no one is watching. I love the way your eyes shift when you smile, the sound of your voice and the way your laugh lingers, the way your hair falls in the morning like it hasn’t yet decided who it belongs to. I love how one way or another you’re getting your daily steps in. I love that face you make when you look at me like I’m a little mad — as if loving you could ever be anything else. And I love how there are so many things you refuse to carry, so many worries you don’t let take up space in your mind. You move through the world lighter than most, and somehow, loving you makes me want to be lighter too. I know I don’t yet know everything about you, but all I want is to learn what remains. If I can love you from only a month and a half of borrowed moments and half-told stories, imagine how much more I could love you once I’ve traced every chapter — once I’ve seen all of you, even the parts you haven’t discovered how to share yet. All I can hope for is one chance — a single, fragile opportunity — to give you everything I have, everything you want, everything you deserve. And if you can’t spare me that chance, I’ll step back and admire you from a distance, hoping you’re happy, hoping you’re at peace, hoping you grow into exactly the person you long to be. And then I’ll love you quietly, carrying it in silence, forever wondering where to lay down all this love I have for you. For I know, deep down, with the depth of what I feel for you, that I won’t easily forget the one who gave me my spark back — the one I gave my heart to, and the one who cost me my mind.

Yesterday I met up with my high school friends here in SM Bicol. It's been a while since we last saw each other because of work, you know, all the traveling and meeting new people. First week of November I attended our monthly meeting at our main office somewhere in North, Region 2. I was fresh from a breakup and decided to install [REDACTED_NAME] to meet someone new, or even just a friend. While I was at the staff house, I matched with this guy from [NCR], an alumni. And, I swear, he was so handsome! Totally my type tall, light skinned, good looking, and smart! We clicked because we both love binge watching series. His opening question on [REDACTED_NAME] was about how many episodes are acceptable to watch in one sitting. That's how our conversation started. He kept asking if he could get to know me better, even suggesting I could sleep at his apartment. Eventually, we decided to meet that night. I was nervous at first, wondering if he might be a catfish or something haha but when we finally met, like ifstg he was real, and even more handsome in person! so, that night, the meet up happened, and he invited me to his super clean apartment. I could tell he was 100% boyfriend material very caring and sweet! I miss him already, hahaha! We decided to cuddle that night, and I made up my mind to stay over since he said it wasn't safe for me to go home so late. But that night, it wasn't just cuddling, it was more than that, but no, walang fck or penetration na naganap because I told myself that I will never ever give my self to someone that is not my boyfriend and I am committed to that promise to myself. While I was on top of him, I could see how happy he looked, he was so handsome! That night, I just wanted to do everything while I was with him. While we were in that position, he asked if it was okay for us to go all the way, but I said no. He asked why, and I told him my reason, and he said he understood. As the night went on, we talked and did more things. While he was on top of me, he asked if I wanted to be his boyfriend. I paused because I knew I wasn't totally healed and I knew he was just carried away with what we were doing, so I said no, and i asked him if he was serious with his question? because he might be carried away with what we were doing, then my phone rang and he said "answer that" it was my coworker, they were telling me to go back to the office, and not to sleep there and go home. I got dressed after we showered together and said goodbye to him. That was the last time I saw him. But before I went home to Bicol, we talked, and he told me to take care on my way home. He also checked up on me after the typhoon hit Bicol. After that, I didn't hear from him again. Yesterday, while I was telling my friend about him, I showed her his pictures from [REDACTED_NAME] because I was so excited to show her how handsome he was! But to my surprise, he had added new photos to his profile, and that's when I realized he wasn't really into me. It just proved that he was just carried away that time with what we were doing. What if I had said yes to his question that night? What if we had become a couple that night, knowing that I would be going back to Bicol soon and I didn't even know when I would be back in the North? Would he be loyal? Or could he commit to a long distance relationship? Hi K, I don't know if you'll ever see this, but as time goes by, I'm getting excited to see you again when I go back to the North. I hope you're still willing to get to know me because I haven't stopped thinking about you since I went back to Bicol L

How hard is it for people to give updates? I have a partner who’s working. He tells me he’s tired and can’t give updates anymore. This has always been our problem, even before we became a couple. Yes, he was still a student back then, but we already had the same issue. I didn’t think it would continue until now because I believed and hoped he would change. We’ve been together for 2 years, and he still hasn’t change. We'll he's trying but he still goes back to his old habits. I’ve been lenient with him—if he can’t update, I try to understand. But sometimes it affects my schedule, and sometimes it hurts when he tells me in the morning that he fell asleep, only for me to find out he was actually doing something else (like gaming, watching something, working, or bonding with family). I understand that those are valid reasons and that it’s his “me time,” but why say he already fell asleep? Instead of being completely honest about it, especially when I always tell him it's okay and I understand. When I approach him about it, he says it’s hard for him to update at night because he’s tired. But my point is, why tell me something you really aren't doing and doesn’t it only take a few seconds to give an update? How hard is it for people to update—especially when you’re in a committed relationship?

I've always wanted to be in UP. I think it's always been a dream, and kind of an inferior complex (and I know it shouldn't be, so on and so forth, etc.) but my dad used to take me to the campus all the time because he was a student there, and I loved the campus, the people, the professors. I didn't get in when I tried for undergrad, that was around early 2000s. But now I got in for a Master's that I can fund by myself. It might sound weird, but it feels like fulfilling a childhood dream. I hope I can do UP justice. I hope I can continue to champion the same values that I've always admired. Salamat UP, for taking a chance on me!

I kindly ask that you keep me anonymous. [REDACTED_NAME], it’s the end of , but my mind’s still stuck on the first day of our music elective in January. Without even knowing your name, you brought the warmth of familiarity with the way you casually asked, “[REDACTED_NAME] ka rin ba?” Our footsteps echoed while we walked through the [DEPARTMENT], student bands practicing in the background. You sang Cigarettes After Sex under your breath, with a warm yet somber tone. That’s when I realized I wanted to know you more. “When words fail, music speaks,” you once told me before finally sharing that you wrote songs. I still feel lucky that you let me hear some on your guitar. You never planned on releasing them, so I assumed they were just letters meant to stay unsent. The intended recipients of these songs remain a mystery, but on those fated nights, the melodies were ours alone. Every hum and pluck felt like they were speaking directly to me. I still wonder if I was the only one who felt the weight of words left unsaid. Mid-semester blues came up, and we stopped talking as much. On one of the few times we did, you said you’d been doing community music therapy sessions held on campus during that time. I heard they’ll be holding more of those sessions soon. I know I shouldn’t, but a part of me still hopes for a chance encounter with you in CMu, just like how we met. Maybe I’ll leave that up to fate. You spoke to me, [REDACTED_NAME], in a way I have never been spoken to before or since. I can only hope that whoever listens to your melodies next gives you the same admiration that you deserve. 🎶 “though ruin itself descended, i’d choose you.” 🎶 Disclaimer: This entry is fictional! Creative, diba? You can expect even more of that creativity in our class’s (real, non-fictional!) Community Music Therapy sessions in Q1 of ! Stay tuned for more. 🙂

Is it me or perhaps the social circles I am in, but leadership feels less like leadership but rather more like an arbitrary role people simply hold. I've just noticed it in groupworks and in orgs but when in a "project" setting, it almost feels TOO lax. As if it wasn't important to finish the job or projects. Tasks aren't followed, workflows are messy, talks are disorganized on what to do and it just feels like there is no sense of direction. These mentioned problems would be fine if they could he solved, but I personally have tried once in a groupwork to be stricter and the leader but it ends up with people resenting or just not getting along with you well. I don't exactly know the balance of strict and lax either and have instead just strayed from taking up leadership positions due to the stress. But again this VERY WELL could just be my outlier of an experience and it isn't as I see it.

Hi, please keep me anonymous. I’m 20F and I’ve been with my boyfriend (same age) for about 4 years now. I just need your thoughts on whether our relationship is still worth continuing. My boyfriend is faithful and caring, he comes from a good family, and we’re both legal with each other’s parents. If those were the only things to consider, I feel like we’d last. But behind all that, it feels like we’re only staying together because of everything we’ve been through. And I think it’s because of my constant issue with him — there’s basically no effort or intimacy. He doesn’t plan dates, he rarely gives flowers or gifts (not even on [REDACTED_NAME]’s, our anniversary, monthsary, or my birthday, not even a letter). He never does anything romantic or sweet, like those little surprises that make you feel special. We started young so he didn’t really go through the whole “ligaw” phase — which is fine, if only I still felt like he’s making an effort to win me over every day. I’ve been thinking about this for years but I always end up brushing it aside, worried that maybe I’m being too materialistic since we’re still students. But then again, you don’t need to spend a lot to make your girl feel appreciated. Please be kind with your thoughts because even I am confused about where our relationship stands. I love him, I really do. And I know he loves me too. But sometimes, I can’t help thinking... maybe I deserve more.

I know people are free to change their minds, but was I that easy to be removed... to be replaced in your life? Never there was a day you weren't in my mind. Your promises that once comforted my fragile heart now haunts me day and night. I was there when you needed me. You were there to hear my woes and worries. We filled each other's cup and share them together. I thought I was someone. Or was I just happened to be anyone? Whoever I was to you eludes me still. Was I never that significant? But you once said that despite everything, at least you have me. How could you say those words? With a voice so sincere, with eyes true to your soul? And then leave me... never to be asked for again. Please tell me why, I will understand and forgive you. You put colors in my life, you were the music I will always want to hear. I will always open my arms for you. I will always bare my soul to you. I will love you again and again. And let you break this heart again... if it's you.

I've read this confession: And I want to break my own silence as well. I graduated with Latin Honours from the UP [DEPARTMENT], once thinking that my degree programme would provide valuable solutions in our social services sector but I was wrong. Opportunities weren't presented to me, instead, I was confronted with the urgent responsibility of paying my family's debt and bills. Like most people, I started as a minimum wage earner and climbed my way past every income bracket. I did corporate work for a field (marketing) I absolutely hated all because that was the job demand; and I was even forced to do things that I am ashamed of for a mere paycheck like sacking an "unproductive" colleague and detecting any "abuse" of the company's benefits system. When I finally told myself that I had enough of pleasing my profit-driven managers and began to side with my co-workers (e.g., allowing them to finish earlier than usual as they had cleared the backlog, writing an appraisal to increase their salaries), I got fired with no due process. I spent the entire noche buena, media noche, and my birthday with barely any food on the table as I grieved the old version of me. I really miss the UP version of me. My depression had only gotten worse as I gradually lost contact with my UP friends who seem to be in a similar plight; we are all struggling to get by in this economy and pervasive government corruption. Unfortunately, I gave up and sought after greener pastures abroad where I was welcomed with better human rights and a labour union that had power over the employer. I currently work in the prison system and am responsible for discharging offenders into the community with social service officers undertaking crime reduction tasks so that these people have another chance at life. Here, PWDs got allowances from the government, the pensions for the elderly are hefty and on-time, job seekers get paid monthly until they are hired, education (tuition) and healthcare are free, paid leaves are worth a month, and various welfare programmes are available on point of need. I'm happy with the work that I do and the money I make, but a part of me still weeps over a dream that has been swept under the carpet. My UP friends are happy for me, but deep down, I want to tell them: you deserve a life like this too. They would ask how I got sponsored for my job, but it's even more painful to tell them that they either need to be lucky or already wealthy. Immigration today is extremely anti-poor and racist; then again, we never have to beg foreign countries to take us in if we had a proper government. To the [REDACTED_DEMONYM] ng Bayan who is reading this, I'll warn you of a harsh reality. At the end of your studies, you will meet a country that is extremely rotten to its core. It has cancer and refuses to heal itself. Those in power will not see you for your merits and good deeds, in fact, intelligent, compassionate and righteous people are punished. Today I climbed a mountain, one that I avoided for years; drank coffee, smoked my red cigarettes, and listened to [REDACTED_NAME]. While doing so I was reading the myth of [REDACTED_NAME] once more. As my lazy eyes toured the cafe, I saw a yellow flower bed. Each of the flowers grew wild, uncontrolled, and probably unkept. I then remember this conversation we had during that sunny day in Pasig. I asked you, about your favorite color, season, and other things that would give me a hint on how I can somehow get closer to you. Through those probing questions, I realized something; just like me — you're just a man. With your own set of insecurities, aspirations, and potential. That moment made me feel like we're just a pair of kids trying to make the most out of it. When you told me didn't like chicken skin, it made me jump out of joy because I'd have more. I was looking forward to eating more meals with you. One of the questions I asked was why is your favorite color yellow. You then answered "because I like sunny days and summer" simple and plain. Through that, you managed to etch your way around my walls. We are polar opposites, we don't have much in common. But, those stolen moments made me want more. You are the opium that made everything I know shake. I swore to myself I'd never let anyone in again, yet here I am, the most blasphemous man — praying to the god I hated the most for a miracle. The lucky streaks changed me, those blue bills went through my head. Nothing is too expensive, it made me believe that I can have the world under my name. I flew too fast by painting the town with blue and red. I lived like a rockstar, wanting everything quickly. The ceilings that seemed so high when I was five is now within arms reach. The faces on those printed papers made me forget yours. Finally, I am winning against the house. I didn't realize the limbo I was in until you left for good. I may have won against the house, but I lost the sun that [REDACTED_NAME] would melt his wax wings for. Maybe in another life we'd get to eat [REDACTED_NAME] on that worn out stand in [NCR] and see those dragon flies again. I love you still. I am sorry.

Way back on August 2, — UPCAT day — something unforgettable happened. That morning, while I was at the Architecture Building, my eyes landed on a girl I didn’t expect to remember so clearly. She was wearing an MNHS lanyard, if I’m recalling it right. But it wasn’t the lanyard that caught my attention — it was her. She looked like an angel quietly passing through the crowd, and for a moment, everything around me slowed down. I didn’t just see a girl; I saw a woman with purpose. There was something about the way she carried herself — the determination in her eyes, the nervous steadiness in her breathing — that made me feel like she was someone who had been praying hard for this moment. Someone who wanted, more than anything, to pass the UPCAT. While we were waiting to be let inside, I watched her from a short distance. She kept murmuring to herself, quietly recalling things, as if reviewing every last detail she had studied for. And in that small moment, I understood: those weren’t just random whispers. Those were pieces of everything she worked for, everything she hoped for, everything she dreamed of. Then the time finally came for us to enter. Before stepping forward, I heard her whisper, “I can do it.” It was simple, but something in her voice made it feel powerful — like a final prayer turned into a promise. I also wanted to pass, of course. But after seeing her, something changed in me. Suddenly, my prayers weren’t just for myself anymore. I found myself silently hoping that she would pass, too — that all her effort, her quiet determination, her whispered affirmations would lead her exactly where she wanted to be. She deserved it. And now, even after that day has passed, I still think about her sometimes. I hope she made it. I hope she’s doing well. I hope our paths cross again someday.

H, you blocked me before I even got to say thank you for always being patient with me and for listening to things I normally wouldn’t share, not even with my closest friends. I know why you chose to cut contact with me, even if you say otherwise. But how was I supposed to know? Were there any other reasons you cut me off besides that? I supposedly wouldn’t know, since you always shut me down. I get it, I made you feel like you were in a roster, but weren’t you also talking to other girls? So what does that make me? I genuinely didn’t know where I stood with you. You say you don’t hate me, but your actions don’t really align with that. I’m sorry for melting down that night, I know it didn’t help. I just can’t fathom how, out of everything I shared with you, that was what made you cut me off in the most excruciating way, especially when I never judged you for your experiences. I hold no grudges, I always wear rose-tinted glasses and I’ll always be grateful for the months we spent talking. You even got me to learn how to smoke yosi, lmao. And considering everything I opened up about, especially the whole drama that went down with my former groupmate, you were my rock back then. Again, thank you. I know you always make fun of my pamahiins, but hopefully when things get weird or whatever, you’ll still keep them in mind. You’ve even answered some of the weirdest questions I ask, hahahahah. Thank you again for everything, and I’m sorry for always deleting my messages when you don’t see them agad. It’s a bad habit, I know. I hope you quit your corpo job and get the chance to open your own gallery or return to making art, because that’s where you belong. As you’ve said, there’s not much lore behind why you took up fine arts; art is simply something you’ve always been doing. I’m not even sure if you’ll see this, since I don’t know if you use other social media platforms because you’re so lowkey, hahaha, but consider this my last message to you. Bye H, and truly, thank you for everything. besos, 🍒🧸

I saw his name again today while scrolling through my old workshop group chat—no reason for my heart to skip, pero nangyari pa rin. Funny how three days with a stranger from another city became something I’m still trying to make sense of month later. I met him in a leadership program we both joined—[REDACTED_NAME], [REDACTED_NAME]. Three days [REDACTED_NAME], [REDACTED_NAME] somehow, he made it feel like the world paused just for us. I didn’t even notice him at first. Day 2, lunch break, pagod na pagod ako so I went back to the dorm room and flopped onto the upper bunk. Then he appeared. Humiga siya sa bed katabi [REDACTED_NAME], [REDACTED_NAME] nandun yung aircon. We started talking—small things, nothing special—but there was this strange comfort between two guys who were practically strangers. That quiet space between our breaths felt strangely safe. When he showed me something on his phone, I leaned closer, and my hand brushed against his. Ayun. Parang [REDACTED_NAME]. “Shett... crush [REDACTED_NAME],” was all my brain could manage. [REDACTED_NAME] nun, I kept stealing glances across the dining hall—[REDACTED_NAME] right table, [REDACTED_NAME] left. Whenever our eyes met, I’d playfully stick my tongue out like “😛” tapos [REDACTED_NAME], pretending I wasn’t silently hoping he noticed me too. May moment pa na super sabog [REDACTED_NAME] siya during parliamentary procedure. When our eyes met again, I made a tiny gesture like, “Ayusin mo hair mo.” And he did. Simple, small, pero ramdam [REDACTED_NAME]. [REDACTED_NAME] siya? [REDACTED_NAME]. Natutunaw ako sa hiya. I acted like he wasn’t even there [REDACTED_NAME] niya na kinakain ako ng crush ko. Day 3 came too soon. [REDACTED_NAME] after that day, life would pull us back into our own worlds—different schools, different cities, different everything. [REDACTED_NAME] magpapicture [REDACTED_NAME], [REDACTED_NAME], [REDACTED_NAME]. And the universe just had to make it worse: pinicture-an ko pa siya... for another girl na friend [REDACTED_NAME] who liked him too. Masakit in a stupid, soft kind of way. Still, I kept it together. Because sometimes, the universe gives you someone not to keep, but just to remember. So this is my public confession. “Mr. [REDACTED_NAME], You don’t know how meaningful the moment we first talked was to me, do you? Maybe you never will, because I don’t think I’ll ever have the courage to tell you. You’ll never know how the warmth of those small moments—our quiet talk, your soft presence, your careless smile—stayed with me in ways I wasn’t prepared for. Thank you for turning a simple 3-day workshop into something unforgettable. You made everything feel lighter without even trying. You made a stranger feel seen. I don’t expect anything. I don’t expect you to remember me, or notice me, or even know that a piece of my heart is still stuck in that cold dorm room where you lay beside me, unaware of the story you accidentally wrote into my life. Wherever you are now, I hope life is gentle with you. Because all I really want is for the boy who made my days brighter—even for a moment—to be happy too. Thank you.”

Hot take for my fellow COS/JO/project-based survivors: Some of you are not “team leads.” You are contractually temporary but emotionally tyrannical. And people are tired. There’s a specific kind of person in research projects: the one who clings to authority they were never actually given, acts like the project contract is a crown, and starts treating every teammate like a subordinate in an imagined hierarchy. Ang papel mo sa TOR? Technical staff. [REDACTED_NAME] galaw mo? Acting dean with unresolved childhood issues. And here’s the real tea: Project-based work breeds confusion, but some people exploit that confusion to justify emotional abuse. Like when someone starts: • dictating routines that aren’t in the contract • policing people’s work styles • manufacturing urgency for ego, not for deliverables • using team GCs as emotional dumping grounds • reacting as if everyone is their emotional support animal • hiding insecurity behind “I’m just being professional” COS ka, not chosen savior of the discipline. What makes it worse: since walang fixed HR structure, people often can’t call it out. Everyone walks on eggshells [REDACTED_NAME], [REDACTED_NAME]-blacklist, [REDACTED_NAME] as “difficult,” [REDACTED_NAME] in a system where funding cycles decide your next meal. So instead of confronting the behavior, the whole team quietly absorbs the emotional turbulence. Secondhand anxiety becomes part of the job description. Trauma bonding disguised as collaboration. And when someone finally reaches breaking point and shuts down for self-preservation? Biglang: “[REDACTED_NAME] effect.” “Bakit ang sensitive?” “[REDACTED_NAME].” Bro, sinabi na: your body language, your tone, your panic loops, lahat yun “sinabi.” Ikaw lang hindi [REDACTED_NAME] too busy convincing yourself that control = competence. No, it’s not. Control without self-awareness is just emotional violence with a planner. And to everyone silently carrying the recoil after moments like that — I do hope it never escalates into the kind of anxiety that manifests physically, the kind that forces someone to cut off and shut down just to stay in peace. Hindi dapat ganun ang trabaho. For anyone reading this who felt that sting: you’re not imagining it. You’re not weak. You’re not “overly sensitive.” Some behaviors genuinely trigger trauma patterns. Some “leaders” are just unhealed kids in adult roles. And some COS titles create more kings than teammates.

Its been a little over a month after she and I separated over a long drawn-out desperate exchange. Long story short, it was nobody's fault. She fell out of love, and it destroyed me. Two years, eleven months, and twenty-seven days gone. Just like that. She was supposed to be the first and the last. It lasts forever, and then it's over. There were a couple of rough weeks after that, and only two people know about our separation (One of my close friends and another acquaintance) but I'm not bitter. It was nobody's fault, after all. If it means anything, we're still friends, although we haven't kept in touch much these days. But still, it hurts. Everything hurts. Now that I'm at this point, I am thoroughly convinced that I will be forever alone. I will live on and keep going, keep doing my own thing. But if someone tries to confess to me or show even remotel interest, I will turn them down. If someone tries to convince me that there might be someone out there for me, I will respectfully call bullshit. Because hope is a mistake. To love is to lose, and I don't want to lose anymore. Because after all, no matter the outcome, "Do it or do not do it: you will regret both."

Love is like solving a system of ordinary differential equations. Except, there isn’t just one or two equations; there are hundreds, and maybe even thousands, that each represent the countless factors that make humans connect: hopes, fears, and dreams. With the seemingly extreme difficulty of this task, how do we even begin to approximate this solution? Like the Runge-Kutta method, we must step carefully through time, and navigate love slowly. We must estimate the slope, predict the next step, and check that errors don’t accumulate. To love, we must iterate and constantly update and understand those we love. Because despite misunderstandings, anger, and doubt, we must believe that our solutions will eventually converge.

Dannie, I hope you never see this... and yet a small part of me wishes you would. There’s something heartbreakingly sweet about loving someone who was never meant to be yours. You walk into a room and everything in me softens, brightens, comes alive—and I have to pretend it doesn’t. I know you’re straight. I know you love someone else. I know the line between us isn’t mine to cross. So I keep these feelings folded tight inside me, trying not to let them spill into the space we share. But the small things you do—the thoughtless kindness, the warmth in your voice, the gentleness in your stares, the smiles that linger just a second too long—they undo me in ways you’ll never notice. They stay with me long after you’ve left. So I love you quietly. Softly. Without asking for anything in return. Grateful just to be near you, even if none of this ever becomes more than a tenderness I carry alone. This isn’t a confession. Just a release. Some loves are meant to be felt, not fulfilled. And mine is one of them. 🩺

I don’t believe in fate, but if I did, I’d say the universe has a twisted sense of humor. Because the first time I meet her, I’m just trying to make it to the train before the doors close. Instead, I crash straight into a girl holding a coffee the temperature of molten hell. She jolts, I grab her wrist on instinct, and for half a second her eyes lock on mine like she’s trying to memorize my face. Then she pulls away. “Sorry,” she says, not really looking at me. Her voice sounds like she’s already somewhere else, somewhere urgent. Somewhere I’m not. I stand there like an idiot watching her run off. It’s fine. People come and go. It’s whatever. Except she doesn’t go. Not really. A week later, she’s at the bookstore I always hide in on slow days. She’s sitting on the floor with a stack of fantasy novels like the world isn’t crumbling outside. She looks up, surprised to see me. I try to play it cool; she tries to act like she remembers my name even though I never told her. We talk. Not much. But enough to make me think about her on the train ride home. Then it keeps happening. The café near my apartment. The street stall selling umbrellas during a storm. The late-night convenience store where she’s squinting at instant ramen like she’s picking a life partner. Each time it gets easier to talk to her. Each time I learn something new. She hates cherries. She laughs quietly, like she’s afraid someone might take it away. She gets this far-off look when she thinks no one’s watching, like she’s imagining a different version of her life. And I, I’m getting attached. Stupidly attached. But I notice things I shouldn’t. Like the way she flinches when her phone lights up with her boyfriend’s name. Like how she says “I’m fine” a little too fast. Like how she never stays in one place long enough to feel safe. And I can’t say anything. It’s not my place. I’m not her person. I’m barely her almost. One night, the shop owner forgets to lock the doors and we get stuck inside after closing. We end up sitting by the window, talking about nothing and everything while the street outside goes dark. She’s close enough that her shoulder brushes mine. Close enough that I swear I can hear her heartbeat. Close enough that I almost..almost..ask her to stay. Instead, I say the dumbest thing possible. “Could’ve been different.” She goes still. No smile. Just this quiet acceptance that hurts more than any rejection could. “Yeah,” she whispers. “In another life.” That’s it. That’s the moment I know we’re not meant to be not because we don’t fit, but because everything around us refuses to. Our hands touch when we stand. A brief, electric mistake I’ll think about for months. We walk out into the night. She goes left. I go right. And I don’t look back.if I do, I’ll ruin us both. But the truth is I feel her absence like a bruise. Slowburn, they’d call it. But some stories aren’t built to last. I'm Chase And this was almost love. Almost.

ba film rant incoming ---> as a third year, i want to say something to my senior [REDACTED_NAME] who side-commented: "[REDACTED_NAME] debriefing [REDACTED_NAME], [REDACTED_NAME]" after our student film prods. don't you get it? this work, prod-work, is both a creative and logistical nightmare. it is ten-fold more unforgiving out there in the professional/commercial world, and an extremely underappreciated line of work. rare is it to last in this industry with genuine passion, and often with calloused tolerance. film school is one of the few spaces where we are allowed to make mistakes, learn from square one, and explore new creative avenues. those who enter this program, while often with the not-so-confident expectation of financial security, also enter this program with a lot of love of the craft they can potentially hone. and postmortems help keep this love fking alive. it's a chance for everyone to feel appreciated, learn new lessons, and further better their skills for future productions. i dont want them to graduate with spite for their industry from the get-go. it's already happening. so, yes, let's get sappy and "waste" time sharing our feelings. because after we graduate, nobody will fking care anymore.

i dont know what it is about you. i dont know know what it is about me either. at this point, im finding it hard to believe that understanding - or spending my waking days trying to - will even amount to anything anymore. i look at you but i dont care for you. i see your face and i feel nothing. i see your body and i feel nothing. vacant eyes remain fixed on my face as i notice the numbers on your socials fluctuate. my inner voice remains silent as I prowl around the life youve subjected to being public. i dont care for you but alas, i still look at you. my eyes still lock onto your obscure silhouette in shadowed rooms. my pupils guard the periphery and grow uneasy in the brightest ones. and the reason? the real reason? i dont know, and i dont think i ever will.

hi, [REDACTED_NAME] i know you like lurking on the [NCR] FW page even though you’re from UST, so maybe you’ll see this—maybe not. but if you do, i hope you read it slowly. i’m sorry for leaving. and the truth is uglier than what i first said: i didn’t leave because my feelings faded. i left because everything around me collapsed so fast that i couldn’t even breathe long enough to choose properly. i was drowning in deadlines and expectations. my body was giving up from the sleepless nights, my grades slipping through my fingers, and underneath all that, there was you. something soft, something warm, something i didn’t want to lose. but they made me choose. and i chose UP. i chose the future i’d been breaking myself for. i chose the dream that was supposed to save me. and then i lost it. i lost UP. i lost you. i lost every version of my future that i thought i was building. and it hurts in a way i don’t know how to say out loud. because i didn’t just lose a 'school', i lost the place i thought i’d grow up in, the life i imagined, the path i sacrificed you for. and you... you were the one thing i tried to protect by walking away. but the moment i let go, everything else followed. so here i am, holding nothing. not you. not UP. just the aftermath of a choice that didn’t save me at all. and the worst part? i still think about what would’ve happened if i held on to you instead. if maybe choosing you would’ve hurt less than losing everything anyway. if maybe keeping you would’ve given me something to come home to after UP slipped out of my hands. i hope to see you again someday. maybe when you’re in med school and i’m in law school somewhere. maybe then we'll finally be able to do to that UPTC date. - matcha P.S. i love matcha too :)

guys, i hope we can stop taking our gfs for granted 😔 she’s always there— supporting you and trying her best to make you feel loved. forgive her if she’s getting clingy or jealous because she’s afraid that having no attention from you will make you forget her. everyday, she’s learning how to be the best girlfriend for you, and i hope you can understand her. you can always communicate. reassurance is the key. if you feel annoyed, you can tell her. don’t be mad. don’t take her for granted. remember, she’s the person who stayed with you through ups and downs. she didn’t leave even when the world tells her to do so because she loves you. let this be an eye opener to be kind and stop pushing people away. love and trust each other. real love is worth fighting for. praying for your better days! -k.

As time passed by you envelop my thoughts more than I want you to, more than what’s healthy. It feels suffocating at times, to constantly be reminded of you in everything I do. I would love to tell you, in fact, my heart just absolutely yearns for it. In another time, another life, I would tell you. I would force the words out of my throat, the sun would shine brighter, the leaves would fall slower, the smile on your face grows even softer. Yet, the moment that only exists in my daydreams. Hey. I'm gonna assume this about me; seeing the timing of other protest-related submissions happening simultaneously. If it isn't, then assume this is a completely unrelated entry. I loved you. Life was so much simpler when the uncertainty of our future was the only thing I worried about. At some point I genuinely believed that I would've done everything that I could for your sake. I didn't know I was capable of such fealty. It scared me. But whatever kindness and temperance you gave was not lost. I guess I've changed. I don't care as much about winning the rat race. I'm not trying to be someone in not. So I'm not the "insecure" partner keeping count anymore. I got better. Though the someone I am now is someone you probably wouldn't be attracted to anymore. But that doesn't matter. I'm finding purpose. My locked apps password is still your birthday and I can't be bothered to change it. My Google Photos has fucking crashed multiple times every time I tried to delete every face of yours in the album. It's buried deep in the trenches. I don't miss you and I doubt you have an organic place in my life anymore. And I can live with no regrets knowing that I gave it my all. It's not my shortcomings. I hope you don't hate me for leaving you. We both deserved so much better. I wish I didn't spoil the friendship we had by wanting something deeper. Something you never could've given me. I wish I hadn't bitten off more than I could chew. I wish my hands weren't too heavy to hold. Or too hot to keep holding onto. But yeah, goodbye. God be with ye. Always have.

Trigger warning: mentions of abuse. To [REDACTED_NAME], CS. There is distance, I am aware of that. And yet, somehow, we find ourselves this evening beside each other in Roxas boulevard, and never more distant than an arm's length. The stillness of Manila’s coasts permitting us this moment, where between us sits your phone playing through its speakers the music you like—our distance now, bound by a volume only meant for us. Sometimes, we break the space; our arms bump, because the road is too narrow to fit us and the traffic, our shoulders press, to push ourselves to safety, and then the touch—your grip on my wrist, as I lead us to cross the street. Your hands barely an inch away from mine, intentionally. We couldn’t dare break that distance. Perhaps the closest we would only ever be. You once confessed to me your injuries; how love marked itself through your bruises—the torture—and his pleasure, from your calls of mercy; the roughness of your calluses to feel how love made you plead, and seeing your avoidance as how such wounds manifest. You're now in [NCR]—an ocean's distance from your past. The time since has amounted to counting itself in seasons, and yet the wounds still stain your words vividly with anguish. We should've known that even the ocean's vastness would be shallow for a pain held closely in memory. I love you, and I cannot be that selfish to ask for romance. You were wounded by love, and you never owed to love what price you paid for it. What matters now is to see your wounds heal—wounds with which no kind of relationship or distance could appease—and I am fine with that. I admit that there have been more than enough times I've thought of wanting to break that little distance between our hands. Perhaps I could offer your calluses the gentleness it craves—or that, I would prefer the seas not allow us its silence if it meant its violent waves would wash your wounds clean. But even the sea itself can't wash away the litter on its own shores—the human trespass to Manila’s coasts; his mortal sin of injuring you. Loving you meant that I have to let go of these delusions. A small concession I am ready to make—a small space—because I don't want to see you injured by love again. jHiZhF3ymp, [REDACTED_NAME] from ECE.

As I walk around campus, I’m constantly reminded that love exists in so many quiet, genuine forms. It’s in the teachers who pour care and patience into everything they do, even on the days when no one seems to notice. It’s in friends who instinctively look out for you, making sure you cross the street safely, sharing a laugh when you need it, or simply showing up without being asked. It’s even in the small, tender moments, like the people who stop for a few seconds just to pet the campus cats, as if offering a tiny piece of kindness to the world. There is love everywhere steady, soft, and sincere. And yet, despite being surrounded by all these forms of love, it often feels like the world places its highest value on romantic love alone. It becomes the centerpiece, the benchmark by which people measure fullness or happiness. And that makes me wonder why so much weight is placed on it, why it seems to overshadow all the other forms of love that are just as real and just as meaningful. Still, I can’t deny that I long for it too. I wonder why something that feels so ordinary for others seems so out of reach for me. Maybe it’s timing, maybe it’s circumstance, or maybe it’s something I haven’t figured out yet. But even in that longing, I recognize that love is not just one thing it’s all around me, in ways I’m still learning to appreciate. And perhaps, someday, the kind I yearn for will find its way to me too. To those who read, I hope you enjoyed my simple food for thought and I hope you find love in the mundane.

I’ve been diagnosed with depression, and I also have a friend who’s going through the same thing. Over time, I found myself slowly distancing from them. I didn't want to, but I knew I had to. I feel bad because we used to be okay, and they’ve always been such a kind person. But at the same time, how can I recover from my own struggles if I’m constantly surrounded by someone else’s pain? They often open up to me about what they’re going through, and while I truly appreciate that they trust me, their sadness eventually started to affect me too. It became overwhelming, so I gradually unfollowed them on social media and eventually deactivated my accounts. We no longer talk. I still feel guilty because I genuinely want to be there for them—but how can I support someone else when I’m barely managing myself? I don’t blame them; it’s not their fault that I’m stuck in this cycle of pain. I am deeply sorry. But still, deep down, I can’t deny that their presence has made my healing a little harder. Even so, I hope we can meet again someday—when I’m mentally stronger and ready to be there for them. I really love this person as a friend, and I hope that when that day comes—when it’s not too late, when you’re still holding on, because I know you’ve been fighting the urge to end it, as well—I hope we can create better, happier memories together. I hope you know that I am still here, praying for you. I really, really love you so much, my friend.

Those who are asking for a reading break due to various reasons, please grow up. College life is a preparation for the 'real life' after college. Working adults only get a mandatory 5 day service leave per year. If you get lucky and work for a top or multinational corporation then maybe you can get 1 month/year of vacation leave. Otherwise, you drag your sick ass to work whether you like it or not. Your longest vacation is a 4-day long weekend during the Holy Week. If Christmas and New Year's day fall on a weekend then tough luck, you are working on Monday morning unless you have approved VL days.

Hi po! Here’s the context of my story: I had a best friend—let’s call her “Z.” She used to be really nice, but after we stopped being classmates, her behavior started to change. She began entertaining multiple guys at once. Even when we told her it wasn't a good idea, she didn’t listen. She just said she was going through her “hoe phase.” She had her first boyfriend, but the relationship didn’t work out. Right after that, she got into a new relationship with a guy I’ll call “B.” Unfortunately, that didn’t work out either because she continued flirting with other guys. That’s why B started to become distant. He even mentioned to his friend that he and Z were on the verge of breaking up. When that news got around, Z assumed they had already broken up—so she decided to make it official. I messaged B, trying to tell him that it might just be a misunderstanding and that maybe they could still fix things with proper communication. But Z was already set on ending things. She said she didn’t feel like she was really in a relationship anyway. Soon after, she found a new boyfriend. Meanwhile, I was comforting B, because I genuinely thought he was a good guy. Over time, we got closer and started hanging out more. Eventually, feelings developed between us. B confessed first, but I took my time thinking about it because of “girl code.” I didn’t want to be the kind of person who breaks that code. But at the same time, it wasn’t my fault that Z had been micro-cheating on B. It’s not like I wanted them to break up, or that I had been waiting for my chance. After a lot of thinking, I decided to reciprocate his feelings—but on one condition: we would keep it lowkey for a while. As for Z, we never really talked again, especially after she found out. I just heard from her new circle of friends that she said it was “weird” that B and I got together, and that we should’ve waited at least three months because of the girl code. But we actually did—B confessed after three months, and we kept the relationship private for almost a year. Now, B and I have been together for three years. I never imagined we’d last this long, but we’re in a healthy relationship and we’re genuinely happy. Sometimes, I feel like Z is jealous or maybe just surprised that B moved on so easily. But isn't it ironic? She’s the one who keeps changing boyfriends—her longest relationship barely lasts a month. I even heard she still dreams about B even while she’s with someone new. I know I silently cut Z off from my life, especially after learning about her cheating issues. But deep down, I sometimes wonder: Was I a bad friend for ending up with her ex? Please be honest with me. Thank you for taking the time to read this.

To my ex-situationship who is in [REDACTED_NAME] now, hi! How are you? I feel like everything ended so abruptly and there were so many things I wish I could’ve said and done to and for you. I know that UP was your dream, and now that you’re there, I really wish I could’ve celebrated that win with you. It’s kind of crazy how we went from updating each other almost everyday and being excited for college life together, to suddenly not speaking to each other at all. If I were being truly honest with myself, I still really miss you and even just the friendship we had. I’m not sure if you still care or not, but if ever you are hesitant about whether I do still care as well, well I do, a lot actually. But I know that you’re happy now, and are making new friends which makes me happy for you too. I hope you know that though yes you did hurt me somehow, I know I had my share of mistakes as well and I know that we BOTH could’ve done better. Also remember that YOU ARE NOT HARD TO LOVE. You never mentioned this to me directly, but to some extent, I understood that sometimes you felt as if you weren’t good enough to receive good things and love from other people. But no, you deserve so so much love, and I even believe that the people who are hard to love are the ones who need love the most. Are you perfect? No. But you are perfectly imperfect, just like every other human. Though us talking again seems unlikely, I genuinely wish you the best. I hope you find success, peace, and happiness in college and that you will get to do the things you told me you were excited to do in UP. May you find someone who will truly love you in the way you want to be loved, and may you find peace as well knowing that you have so so much to live for. I didn’t get to say this to you before, but I really love and appreciate you : )

It is currently 2 in the morning, and I can't help but think. I'm tired from the day and very sleepy, plus I gotta wake up in the morning to do my laundry and do my GE term paper. But, for some weeks now, I can't stop thinking about her. I'm back to my old self for her—no hatred, nothing like that. I can't stop thinking of her tonight. I miss her; I wonder what she's up to, what she's doing, and all that. I hate myself for this kase ang simp [REDACTED_NAME], ayoko nun, bullshit for me. But what can a man do? They say first loves don't die. I am afraid it is really true. Can I still find love aside from her? Why am I looking for her? To validate these feelings ba? Lately, I started just taking all these feelings and thoughts in—hindi na iiwasan [REDACTED_NAME]. I learned that I have to take agency for what I'm doing and thinking, maybe even feeling. I gotta be accountable for all of it. Lahat ng negatives iniwasan [REDACTED_NAME] all these years at [REDACTED_NAME] kinonfront, even kung ano mang nararamdaman ko pa for her kase ayokong isipin, ayokong damdamin. It's almost three years since we broke up, and ngayon tinatry [REDACTED_NAME] bang iniiwasan [REDACTED_NAME] all these years—take it in at damdamin iyon. I just miss her—her smile, her voice, the way she looked, the way she presented herself, the way she smelled, and even the stink of her breath (weirdly enough). I miss her almond eyes with their dark iris whose gaze pierces through and intimidates many people, only to find out how charming she really was once they got to know her. She's honestly just a normal woman, but she looked sooo extraordinary to me. Every time I pass by near her place, I'm always on the lookout for her, just to have a glimpse of her face. I kept doing this for the past three years, even if those were the times that I've come to hate her as well, although to no avail. I look for her in even the most unexpected places, hoping that that 0.001% of odds of finding her there will work. Right now, I'm just so powerless to do anything anymore. I often thought of how I'd feel if I ever saw her with another man, and back then, when we were still together, I always told her that I'd feel devastated. Pag iniisip [REDACTED_NAME] now, I feel apathetic, like I feel like I could handle it fairly alright, but a part of me strongly suggests that I can't and, like what I've said before, it will devastate me. My mind has given up, but my heart still holds hope. War is waged inside me between these two. The heart wants to stay, but the mind asks how to do that in this case and strongly suggests to go. I hope none of the bois and younger siblings go through this. I hope they could take care of the love that they have, cultivate it, protect it, and weather through storms with it.

Hi! I hope you’re all doing well. I don’t usually write or post messages like this here. I wish I could confront my classmate in person, but I don’t have the courage to say these things to him, my peers, or my family. I almost didn’t think this post mattered, because I know there are students facing bigger challenges than mine. Why I’m here isn’t really important, I think. But this has been bothering me since second year (yes I’m still classmates with him now, in my fourth year). Note: I’ve changed some details to protect my privacy. I’m scared he might find out. (Yes I used AI to help me paraphrase to keep my identity hidden). It started with what seemed like harmless “jokes.” He would say things like, “Bakit ang simple [REDACTED_NAME]? Ang pangit may [REDACTED_NAME]. [REDACTED_NAME] social life.” At first, I laughed it off because I thought we were just joking around. I know I can be sensitive, and I accept that. But there was a time when he went too far, and it triggered a relapse in my mental health. I was paired with him and another classmate for a presentation. Right before presenting, we made a lot of last-minute changes—changes I wasn’t told about, especially for my part. He said out loud, “Are you stupid? Next time, don’t be annoying. I’m frustrated with you because it seems like you don’t know anything.” I cried so much, though thankfully no one noticed. After that, I was triggered and had an attempt (I think you know what I mean. I was admitted to a hospital) . Lately, I think I’m doing a bit better. But he still affects me deeply. Another time, when something awful happened to me (long story) my classmates didn’t know what to say and tried to be careful because some knew about my mental health. But he called me out in front of everyone, saying, “[REDACTED_NAME]. [REDACTED_NAME] okay [REDACTED_NAME]”. His laugh made me cry, and I was absent the next day. I’m aware that I have certain privileges, but I’ve never flaunted them. I’ve never been mean to him—I even gave him medicine and water when he had a fever. I don’t know how to confront him. I feel too small. I don’t know who I can reach out to. I’m afraid of being mocked. Every time I try to reach out or speak up, I feel scared. If you think I’m being childish for sharing this, Its okay I understand and like I said not really a big issue naman. To those who read this, thank you. Even if you could have been reading something else, I appreciate that you read my story. :)

November 10, I am in Quezon City, heartbroken as I watch my family in the province struggle through Typhoon Uwan. Our home was always a sanctuary, shielded by three mountains that kept us safe even from Signal No. 3 typhoons. But the updates I'm getting now are terrifying. This is different; this is the strongest one yet. I cannot see the logic of the people in government. Flood control systems? What a joke. These are just projects you use to steal money from the very people you swore to protect. You speak of fixing the system, but the system is broken beyond simple repair. That would take decades. What you should be fixing right now are the natural resources you have neglected for so long. I hope your conscience haunts you as you lie in your comfortable beds, drowning in money, while your people are outside, drowning in floodwaters. PHILIPPINES IS COMPOSED OF A BROKEN SYSTEM, IN ALMOST EVERY ASPECTS. You are already broken. Do not take our natural resources down with you, especially when you can't even protect us from the calamities and your "helping ghost projects" even makes it worse. -seeghie

The best thing that happened in UP. I hate this mlm type of thing. I’m 18 and he’s 17. We met this year since we’re in the same course. He’s tall, religious, and the kind of person you’d easily call a green flag. We share the same goals, the same dreams, the same kind of self-awareness that made me think maybe, for once, I met someone who truly understands me. But he’s straight. And he doesn’t know I like him. I think it’s better that way. He once told me his past relationships didn’t work out because he wanted something long-term, the kind of love that ends in marriage. And I know I can’t give him that, no matter how much I wish I could. Every time he talks about those failed relationships, I just sit there, smiling, pretending that it doesn’t hurt. But deep down, I’m thinking that he has no idea how much I wanted that kind of love too. It’s just harder for people like me. Finding an mlm connection that’s pure, grounded, and filled with the same values and dreams feels almost impossible sometimes. I’ve never been in a relationship. Still have my V card. Dating has never really been part of my world. But meeting him made me realize how beautiful it is to connect with someone on a deeper level. Even if it’s not returned, even if it’s not meant to last, it still means something. And honestly, dating culture here in Manila feels exhausting. Everything moves too fast, too surface-level. So if ever you’re reading this, I just want you to know that there are still people who look beyond appearance, who see worth in shared dreams, values, and principles. If you don’t see me next year, it’s probably because I’ve transferred. We both know why I came to UP and the passions I still have to chase. I just hope that someday you find the woman who truly deserves you. I just needed to let this out. I’m not going to confess. When I leave, I plan to quietly cut all connections with the people I met here. Maybe this is my way of saying goodbye, without actually saying it. P.S. [REDACTED_NAME] everyone sa current typhoon. (⁠╥⁠_⁠╥⁠)

chat, have you ever felt like this before? picture this. you and a friend of yours have really hit it off ever since you guys met. the humor, the banter, the comfort – it's all there. you start to think you could really like this guy. but then you also find out he already has a girlfriend. and that's fine! your feelings hadn't even run deep at that point, and you leave the issue alone. you decide not to pursue it. you're able to contain yourself, and the friendship is as good as ever. you're satisfied and genuinely happy. but come late nights, you can't help but think about it – what if? you wouldn't do it, of course. you know you shouldn't and you have no intention of doing so. but deep down, you know you would fold if he asked. and you hate yourself for it. chat, am i the only one? I'm now eating there The places where we used to eat But there were habits that were hard to unlearn Even after a month of dinners without you At Ate Fe's, I accidentally ordered extra rice with my lechon rice You were always in need of it I just took it home for breakfast At Jollibe, I ordered my C4 with fries even though I hated them You always ate them for me I eat them now with disdain You shunned me for my sheer hatred of fries I sneered at you when you didn't like broccoli The extra rice I have stored in my fridge Now sit with that brand of beer we used to banter with The games we played at Sefali's The dreams we planned while scouring A2 For a while, they reminded me of what we were The places where we shared our hunger It tastes the same.
